name: Chengdu
tagline: Slow Down. Eat More. See Pandas.

attractions.name: Giant Panda Research Base
attractions.tip: Go at 8am opening — pandas most active before 10am. Baby pandas are in the nursery (月亮产房). Book via WeChat app or official website — walk-ins turned away on weekends and holidays. The red panda section is often overlooked but equally adorable.

attractions.name: Wuhou Shrine + Jinli Street
attractions.tip: 1,800 years of Three Kingdoms history. Go early — the red wall bamboo corridor at 7am is extraordinary. Exit into Jinli Street for street food but skip the main drag and head to the back lanes (锦里后街) for three-cannon sticky rice (三大炮) and Zhang Fei beef.

attractions.name: Kuanzhai Alley (Wide & Narrow Alleys)
attractions.tip: Three parallel Qing dynasty alleyways. Go before 9am for peaceful atmosphere. Ignore the main street tourist shops — duck into Well Lane (井巷子) for local snacks and the preserved old city wall section. The side gate off Kuan alley has the best old building views.

attractions.name: People's Park & Heming Teahouse
attractions.chinese: 人民公园 & 鹤鸣茶社
attractions.tip: The soul of Chengdu. Sit in a bamboo chair at Heming Teahouse (¥16 for bottomless gaiwan tea), watch elderly locals play cards and fan dance. Get an ear cleaning (采耳, ¥30) — a Chengdu-only experience. The matchmaking corner (相亲角) nearby is fascinating to observe.

attractions.name: Taikoo Li + IFS Climbing Panda
attractions.tip: The giant panda climbing IFS mall is Chengdu's most viral landmark. Best photo angle: stand on street level looking up. Taikoo Li opposite is the most beautiful mall in China — traditional Sichuan architecture wrapping luxury brands. Best visited after 7pm when lit up.

attractions.name: Dufu Thatched Cottage
attractions.tip: Former home of Tang dynasty poet Du Fu. The bamboo groves and pavilions feel genuinely ancient. The 'Poet's Plum Blossom Trail' outside the paid zone is free and lovely. Visit in rain for the most poetic atmosphere — exactly the setting Du Fu described.

attractions.name: East District Memory (Dong Jiao Ji Yi)
attractions.tip: Former Soviet-era electronics factory turned music and art district. The 'Chengdu' wall mural is the most viral photo spot. Live concerts most weekends. The Time Post Box (时光邮局) lets you mail a letter to yourself in 5 years — beautiful concept.

attractions.name: Jinsha Site Museum
attractions.tip: 3,000-year-old Shu Kingdom site with extraordinary gold and jade artefacts — including the Sun and Immortal Birds gold foil, now China's cultural heritage symbol. Far less crowded than Wuhou Shrine. Book the joint ticket with Sanxingdui for a discount.

attractions.name: Sanxingdui Museum
attractions.tip: The most mysterious archaeological site in China — 3,000-year-old bronze masks with huge protruding eyes found in 1929, origin still unknown. New museum opened 2023. 1.5hrs from Chengdu. The bronze 'tree of life' is truly unlike anything else. Book weeks ahead in high season.

attractions.name: Qingcheng Mountain (Front Range)
attractions.tip: One of Taoism's four sacred mountains, 1hr from Chengdu. Dense cloud forest, Taoist temples and a cable car. The 'sutra copying for free accommodation' activity (抄经换宿) at the Taoist academy is limited to 10 spots daily — book via their WeChat. The back mountain (后山, ¥20) has better scenery and fewer crowds.

attractions.name: Dujiangyan Irrigation System
attractions.tip: 2,300-year-old water diversion system still in daily use — a UNESCO World Heritage engineering marvel. Walk across the ancient rope bridge (安澜索桥). Combine with Qingcheng Mountain on the same day (15 min drive between them).

attractions.name: Sichuan Opera (Face Changing Show)
attractions.tip: Performers change elaborate painted masks in under a second — genuinely impossible to track how. The Shufeng Yayun teahouse show includes tea service and snacks. Book front-row seats. The fire-spitting act that opens the show is spectacular.

attractions.name: Yulin Road & Local Nightlife
attractions.tip: Zhao Lei's famous song 'Chengdu' is about this street. An authentic local neighbourhood with indie cafes and bars that hasn't been touristified. Start on Yulin North Road (玉林北路), wander into the side lanes. The small bar Jiu Guanjia is a local institution since 1996.

attractions.name: Leshan Giant Buddha (Day Trip)
attractions.tip: World's largest stone Buddha at 71m — carved into a cliff face over 90 years (713–803 AD). Book the ferry boat view (¥70) for the full frontal perspective you've seen in photos. The cliff-face stairs descend right past the Buddha's feet. Get there before 9am.

attractions.name: Chengdu Research Base Night Tour
attractions.tip: New night tour at the panda base — see red pandas and nocturnal animals under lantern light. Very limited tickets, book weeks ahead via official app. Completely different experience from the daytime visit. The full moon in September is a special event.

attractions.name: Huang Long Xi Ancient Town
attractions.tip: 1,700-year-old water town 40km from Chengdu — much less visited than Wuzhen. Ancient streets, fire dragon lantern festival (正月) and a famous fresh fish market. Worth it for the peaceful riverside teahouses alone. Free, unlike most ancient towns.

attractions.name: Tianfu Art Park
attractions.tip: Brand new 2023 world-class arts complex — Chengdu's answer to M+ in Hong Kong. Three museums, massive public sculpture garden and lake. The architecture alone is worth the trip. Free outdoor areas are spectacular. Museum tickets ¥20–80 depending on exhibition.

attractions.name: Tomb of Emperor Wang Jian (Yongling)
attractions.tip: The only surviving above-ground royal tomb in China from the Five Dynasties period (907–925 AD) — an actual emperor's burial chamber you walk into. Low-key and rarely crowded. The stone musicians carved on the tomb are extraordinary works of art.

food.name: Mapo Tofu (Original)
food.where: Chen Mapo Tofu (陈麻婆豆腐) — Qingyang district, original since 1862
food.tip: The original, invented in Chengdu in 1862. Silky tofu in numbing chilli and minced pork sauce — the '麻' (numbing) must come from Sichuan pepper, not just chilli. Chen's is the original location. Order extra rice.

food.name: Dan Dan Noodles
food.where: Zhang Laoer Liangfen stalls or Jinli Street back lanes
food.tip: Thin noodles in sesame paste, chilli oil, minced pork and Sichuan pepper. The name comes from the shoulder poles (担) vendors carried. Best eaten standing at a street stall for the full experience.

food.name: Chengdu Hotpot
food.where: Piao Xiang Hotpot (飘香火锅) — local favourite since the 1990s
food.tip: Slightly different from Chongqing hotpot — more fragrant, less brutally numbing. The bone broth base is a Chengdu hallmark. Must-try: 鸭肠 (duck intestines) flash-cooked for exactly 7 seconds. Always order 毛肚 (tripe).

food.name: Kung Pao Chicken (Original)
food.where: Traditional Sichuan restaurants in Wuhou district
food.tip: The original Kung Pao Chicken is a Sichuan dish — nothing like the Westernised version. Diced chicken with dried chillies, peanuts and Sichuan pepper in a sweet-sour-spicy lychee-flavour sauce. Should be slightly smoky from wok hei.

food.name: Three Cannon Sticky Rice
food.where: Jinli Street back lanes — look for the theatrical stall
food.tip: Glutinous rice balls thrown onto a metal plate making a cannon sound (三大炮). Covered in sesame, red bean powder and brown sugar syrup. As theatrical as it is delicious.

food.name: Rabbit Head
food.where: Kuanzhai Alley area stalls and night markets
food.tip: Chengdu's most famous adventurous street food — whole rabbit head in spicy sauce. Split with chopsticks, eat the cheek meat and brain. Locals eat these like Singaporeans eat chicken wings. Very addictive, zero guilt.

food.name: Longchaoshou (Dragon Wonton)
food.tip: Chengdu's most iconic wonton shop, founded 1941. The wontons are silkier and thinner-skinned than northern versions. Original broth (清汤) or red oil (红油) dressing. The set meal with dandan noodles is the local classic order.
